hero.title: Scouts des Cèdres
hero.subtitle: Saint Jean Marc Group - Byblos
hero.motto: “Just as our ancestors spread the alphabet, we will spread the scout movement.”

about.title: Why Scouting?
about.tagline: Building the Leaders of Tomorrow, Today

general.title: What is Scouting?
general.text1: Scouting is a voluntary, non-political educational movement for young people, open to all without distinction. It offers youth the opportunity to develop their full physical, intellectual, emotional, social, and spiritual potentials as individuals and responsible citizens.
general.text2: Through outdoor activities, group collaboration, and community service, scouts learn life-saving skills, independence, and the value of helping others.

history.title: Saint Jean Marc Group
history.text1: Based in the historic city of Byblos (Jbeil), Lebanon, the Saint Jean Marc Group (فوج سان جان مارك) has been guiding young minds under the umbrella of the 'Scouts des Cèdres' association. Connected to the historic St. John Mark Cathedral, we teach youngsters to cherish their heritage while sailing toward a bright future.
history.text2: Our group is shaped by the ancient Phoenician maritime spirit: adventurous, resilient, and dedicated to bridging cultures.

sections.title: Our Scout Sections
sections.subtitle: Tailored programs matching every age group's growth and development.

louveteaux.title: Louveteaux
louveteaux.description: Through playful outdoor exploration, stories, and cooperative games, young boy scouts build confidence, learn group sharing, and explore the outdoors safely.

jeannettes.title: Jeannettes
jeannettes.description: Focused on encouraging young girls to develop self-reliance, physical fitness, and outdoor survival skills through guided, creative play.

eclaireurs.title: Boy Scouts
eclaireurs.description: Focused on campcraft, hiking, teamwork, and patrol structures. Scouts take on responsibilities, master pioneering skills, and lead their first mini-expeditions.

guides.title: Guides
guides.description: Developing independence, outdoor skills, and leadership for young girls through teamwork and patrol systems.

routiers.title: Routiers
routiers.description: Empowering young men to design community service projects, undertake major wilderness treks, and develop personal philosophies focused on active citizenship.

caravelles.title: Caravelles
caravelles.description: Fostering active citizenship, leadership, and social responsibility for young women through community service and adventure planning.

chefs.title: Chefs & Cheftaines
chefs.description: Dedicated volunteers who coordinate activities, ensure safety, and guide younger scouts, carrying the group's Phoenician heritage forward.

agenda.title: Upcoming Activities
agenda.subtitle: Follow our path: meetings, campings, and community actions.

gallery.title: Moments in Action
gallery.subtitle: A glimpse into our campfires, hikes, and gatherings in Jbeil.

testimonials.title: What They Say About Us
testimonials.subtitle: Voices from our parents, alumni, and community leaders.

items.quote: Scouting at St. Jean Marc has transformed my son. He went from being shy to leading his own patrol, learning responsibility and respect for nature.
items.author: Marcelle A.

items.quote: The friendship, discipline, and Lebanese heritage I learned in this group stay with me daily. The Phoenicican ship motto is not just a slogan; it defines our character.
items.author: Elio G.

items.quote: A safe, positive environment where children learn values that are hard to find elsewhere. Highly recommended for every family in Byblos.
items.author: Rita K.

faqs.subtitle: Got questions about fees, schedules, or safety? We have answers.
